threads.topic: Wideband Firmware Update Issue
threads.summary: User @tmbryhn encountered an issue while updating wideband firmware using a SD card on the rusEFI/epicEFI system. The update process failed and left the wideband controller non-responsive ("bricked"). However, restoring functionality with ST-LINK was possible.
key_details: Directory for firmware file must be correct
Filename should not include ".bin" extension visible in the directory
Update process reports "Busy"
Controller becomes non-responsive after power cycle
Restoration of wideband function is possible using ST-LINK
threads.raw_summary:
TOPIC: Wideband Firmware Update Issue
CONTRIBUTORS: @ggurov, @tmbryhn
SUMMARY: User @tmbryhn encountered an issue while updating wideband firmware using a SD card on the rusEFI/epicEFI system. The update process failed and left the wideband controller non-responsive ("bricked"). However, restoring functionality with ST-LINK was possible.
KEY_DETAILS:
- Directory for firmware file must be correct
- Filename should not include ".bin" extension visible in the directory
- Update process reports "Busy"
- Controller becomes non-responsive after power cycle
- Restoration of wideband function is possible using ST-LINK

threads.topic: Engine not returning to crank mode after starting
threads.summary: User @ggurov reported an issue where the engine does not return to the cranking state after starting. Developer @epicEFIdev suggested checking the settings related to the fuel pump and ignition timing in the rusEFI/epicEFI software.
key_details: Check fuel pump and ignition timing settings
Ensure proper transition logic is implemented in the code

threads.topic: Flashing issues leading to bricked Wideband (WB) Controller
threads.summary: User @ggurov encountered an issue where flashing a file caused their Wideband (WB) Controller to stop functioning. According to @tmbryhn, this specific problem affects only the WB MCU after certain updates and does not impact the ECU firmware itself. A full chip erase and re-flash using ST-LINK resolves the issue.
key_details: WB controller stops connecting post SD update
Issue also occurs with "2025" update in TS without SD card
Only the WB MCU is affected, not the ECU firmware
Solution involves a full chip erase and re-flash with ST-LINK
threads.raw_summary:
TOPIC: Flashing issues leading to bricked Wideband (WB) Controller
CONTRIBUTORS: @ggurov, @tmbryhn
SUMMARY: User @ggurov encountered an issue where flashing a file caused their Wideband (WB) Controller to stop functioning. According to @tmbryhn, this specific problem affects only the WB MCU after certain updates and does not impact the ECU firmware itself. A full chip erase and re-flash using ST-LINK resolves the issue.
KEY_DETAILS:
- WB controller stops connecting post SD update
- Issue also occurs with "2025" update in TS without SD card
- Only the WB MCU is affected, not the ECU firmware
- Solution involves a full chip erase and re-flash with ST-LINK
